questions.question: What is the data type of a one-dimensional array?
options: A) Linked List
B) Circular Linked List
C) One-Dimensional Array
D) Multi-Dimensional Array
questions.explanation: A one-dimensional array is a linear sequence of elements of the same data type stored in contiguous memory locations.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the '}' character in an array declaration?
options: A) To specify the size of the array
B) To specify the data type of the array
C) To specify the scope of the array
D) To specify the end of the array declaration
questions.explanation: The '}' character is used to specify the end of the array declaration.

questions.question: What is a two-dimensional array?
options: A) An array of arrays
B) A linked list of arrays
C) A data structure that stores multiple arrays in a single block of memory
D) A circular linked list of arrays
questions.explanation: A two-dimensional array is a data structure that stores multiple arrays in a single block of memory.

questions.question: How do you declare a one-dimensional array with a specified size?
options: A) using the keyword 'int';
B) using the keyword 'int[10];'
C) using the keyword 'int array[10];'
D) using the keyword 'int array = 10;'
questions.explanation: To declare a one-dimensional array with a specified size, use the keyword 'int[10];'.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'size' parameter in a two-dimensional array declaration?
options: A) To specify the data type of the array
B) To specify the scope of the array
C) To specify the size of the array
D) To specify the end of the array declaration
questions.explanation: The 'size' parameter is used to specify the number of rows in a two-dimensional array.
